---
title: "UBCO Freshwater Science"
subtitle: "Course pathways, designations, and learning outcomes"
date: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Land acknowledgement
The UBC Okanagan campus is situated within the unceded, ancestral, and
traditional territory of the **Syilx Okanagan Nation**. The Freshwater
Science program, its teaching, its research, its applied work in
freshwater systems, depends on this land and on relationships with
the Indigenous Peoples and rights-holders who continue to steward it.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## About this resource
This is a curated **advising and planning resource**, maintained by the
program lead. **The
[UBC Okanagan Academic Calendar](https://okanagan.calendar.ubc.ca/)
is the authoritative source for all degree requirements, course
prerequisites, and program rules.** Whenever this site and the calendar
disagree, the calendar wins. Always confirm your degree plan with the
program advisor.
:::

<!--
  Watershed photo gallery + advisor headshot land here once the
  image files are saved into assets/photos/. CSS in assets/styles.css
  is already styled for the .watershed-gallery and the .contact-card
  .contact-card-photo class. Markup to drop back in:

  ::: {.watershed-gallery}
  <figure><img src="assets/photos/skaha-okfalls.jpg" alt="..."><figcaption>...</figcaption></figure>
  ...four figures total...
  :::
-->

# A planning resource for current and prospective students

Welcome to the planning hub for the **Freshwater Science** undergraduate
program at the [UBC Okanagan campus](https://ok.ubc.ca/). This site
helps you:

- **Browse the courses** that count toward Freshwater Science, with
  current-year prerequisites, equivalencies, and offering status.
- **Plan a path** to the **PAg** (BC Institute of Agrologists) or
  **RPBio** (College of Applied Biology) professional designation
  alongside your degree.
- **Track learning outcomes** at the program, department, and
  individual course level.
- **See where each skill is scaffolded** across the four-year arc
  (from "intro to R" to "designing a field study").
- **Use the interactive planner** to test course combinations against
  your degree requirements and designation goals.

## Quick starts

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-md-6 .g-col-12}

### I'm a current student
- The interactive [Planner](planner/index.qmd) is in development;
  preview the design intent on its placeholder page.
- Check the [PAg pathway](designations/pag.qmd) and [RPBio
  pathway](designations/rpbio.qmd) to see what gaps you still need
  to fill before graduation.
- Browse the [course catalogue](courses/index.qmd) for prerequisites
  and "is this offered next year?" signals.
- The department's official page lives at
  [eesc.ok.ubc.ca → Freshwater Science](https://eesc.ok.ubc.ca/undergraduate/freshwater-science/).

:::

::: {.g-col-md-6 .g-col-12}

### I'm a prospective student
- Read the official program description at
  [UBC Undergraduate Admissions → Freshwater Science](https://you.ubc.ca/ubc_programs/freshwater-science/).
- Skim the [program overview](#about-the-program) below.
- Compare the [designation pathways](designations/pag.qmd) to see what
  professional qualifications this degree can lead to.
- Browse the [first-year courses](courses/index.qmd#year-1) to get a
  feel for the workload.

:::

:::

## About the program

The Freshwater Science program integrates aquatic biology, chemistry,
geography, and earth and environmental sciences. Students study water
quality and quantity, aquatic organisms, and the health of aquatic
ecosystems.

The four-year B.Sc. requires 120 credits with at least 78 in Science,
at least 42 at the upper level (300+), and at least 36 of those
upper-level credits in Science. The Honours track adds a research
thesis (EESC_O 449).

**Official UBCO pages**:

- [UBC Undergraduate Admissions, Freshwater Science](https://you.ubc.ca/ubc_programs/freshwater-science/)
  (program overview for prospective students)
- [Department of Earth, Environmental and Geographic Sciences, Freshwater Science](https://eesc.ok.ubc.ca/undergraduate/freshwater-science/)
  (department page; current-student facing)
- [UBC Academic Calendar, Freshwater Science requirements](https://okanagan.calendar.ubc.ca/faculties-schools-and-colleges/faculty-science/bachelor-science-programs/major-programs/freshwater-science)
  (authoritative degree requirements)

::: {.callout-note}
## Source of truth
Course descriptions and prerequisites are pulled daily from the
[UBC Okanagan Academic Calendar](https://okanagan.calendar.ubc.ca/).
Designation requirements are curated from the
[BCIA UBCO Approved Course List](https://www.bcia.com/) and the
[CAB Credentialing Standard](https://cab-bc.org/) by the program lead.
The [Methodology page](docs/methodology.qmd) details the data
pipeline and refresh cadence.
:::

## Talk to your advisor

::: {.contact-card}
::: {.contact-card-body}
### Dr. Nelson Jatel, PAg
Freshwater Science Program Advisor, UBC Okanagan

Email for advising appointments, course planning, designation
questions, Honours / Co-op / NSERC USRA enquiries, or anything else.

<a class="contact-card-cta" href="mailto:nelson.jatel@ubc.ca?subject=UBCO%20Freshwater%20Science%20-%20Student%20enquiry">📧 nelson.jatel@ubc.ca</a>
:::
:::

## Who maintains this

Curated and maintained by **Dr. Nelson Jatel, PAg**, Lecturer and
Freshwater Science Program Advisor, Department of Earth, Environmental
and Geographic Sciences, UBC Okanagan. For advising appointments or to
flag a correction, email
[nelson.jatel@ubc.ca](mailto:nelson.jatel@ubc.ca?subject=UBCO%20Freshwater%20Science%20-%20Student%20enquiry).

Source code is open and at
[github.com/DrNelsonJatel/UBCO_Freshwater_Science_Program](https://github.com/DrNelsonJatel/UBCO_Freshwater_Science_Program).
Issues and suggestions welcome via the GitHub issues tracker.

### Status of this resource (as of last update)

| Section | Status |
|---|---|
| Course catalogue | **Live data**, scraped daily from the UBC Okanagan Academic Calendar |
| Designations (PAg) | **Live data**, rendered from the [BCIA UBCO approved-course list](https://www.bcia.com/sites/default/files/docs/resources/UBCO%20Approved%20Courses_June%2027,%202025.pdf) (June 2025) |
| Designations (RPBio) | **Curated** by the program advisor from the [CAB Credentialing Standard](https://cab-bc.org/wp-content/uploads/Credentialing_Standard_June-2025.pdf) (June 2025), CAB does not publish a UBCO-specific list |
| Program Learning Outcomes | **Curated** from the v2 PLO memo (28 April 2026) |
| Complementary pathways | **Curated** from the program advisor's pathway map |
| Curriculum-map matrix (PLOs × courses) | **In development**, EESC 301 pilot only; instructor interviews pending |
| Skills scaffolding | **In development**, draft vocabulary only |
| Interactive Degree Planner | **In development**, see [Planner page](planner/index.qmd) for the design intent |
